sources.notes: Final hard combinatorial counting problem in the Counting Is Fun series.
Official tutorial gives a long combinatorial derivation with implementation-heavy case handling.

annotation.summary: Count the requested class of objects under several structural constraints.
annotation.constraints: The intended solution is formula/DP based; direct enumeration is impossible at the stated limits.
annotation.core_idea: The final count is decomposed by the key structural parameter from the statement. For each parameter value, the remaining choices split into independent combinatorial factors. Inclusion/exclusion of invalid boundary cases and careful modular arithmetic produce the final expression.
annotation.complexity: O(n log n) or O(n) after precomputing factorials and prefix sums.

solution_variants.name: case-split-combinatorial-formula
solution_variants.summary: Evaluate the official case-split formula using factorial and prefix-sum precomputations.

tags.evidence: The main solution is a derived combinatorial count with factorial/binomial-style terms.
Invalid boundary/case overlaps are subtracted from the main count.
The official solution has many formula cases and modular precomputations to implement correctly.
